author: claude

patch.value: The 'Verify bun install of published package' step was fatal (5x30s then exit 1) and ordered before 'Create GitHub release', so bun registry-mirror lag (worst for prerelease -N versions) could block the real release after npm publish + tag + changelog commit already succeeded. This actually blocked pm-changelog 2026.6.13-1's GitHub release. Fleet-wide propagation of the pm-changelog#49 fix (npm-authoritative fallback).
Harden release bun-verify so registry-mirror lag cannot block the GitHub release
Make the Daily Release resilient to bun registry-mirror lag so a cosmetic verify failure never blocks the actual GitHub release.

bun-verify retries 8x then falls back to authoritative 'npm view pkg@version'; npm-confirmed publishes emit ::warning:: and exit 0 so Create GitHub release runs; only a genuinely-absent npm version fails the job; CI green; no secrets.
release.yml verify step exits 0 on npm-confirmed publishes despite bun mirror lag; GitHub release is created.

value.author: claude
value.text: Identical release.yml verify block across all active pm repos; same deterministic transform applied.

value.author: claude
value.text: See pm-changelog PR #49 for the lead fix and full rationale.

value.author: claude
value.text: Calendar -N suffixes are semver prereleases; bun resolves them via a registry mirror that lags npm publish,making the verify smoke test the flakiest for -N releases.

message: Propagate fleet-wide CI release-pipeline hardening (lead: pm-changelog pmc-pr0j / PR #49)
